reason: Kani-harness functions run under cfg(kani) to call kani:: functions; production builds do not include the harness body.
impact: honest code-only proof (proptest shadow produces property-class coverage without requiring kani)
compensating_evidence: proptest v15.OBL-P3-KANI-ID-PROPTEST; test runs both with and without cfg(kani).

reason: Cargo-kani is invoked through systemd-run to enforce cgroup memory cap. Trust boundary is the host systemd behavior.
impact: kernel-level cgroup limits memory and swap; per-harness run terminates the lane on OOM (not the host).
compensating_evidence: PROOF_KANI_BLOCKED finding emitted on cgroup exit; lane stays green unless every harness blocks.

reason: Cargo-mutants' success/caught/timeout/unviable classification is trusted at the boundary.
impact: a future cargo-mutants version that misclassifies would silently shift the gate posture.
compensating_evidence: MutantsBaseline::load returns typed MutantsBaselineError::SchemaVersion if a future version's JSON schema diverges.

reason: MutantsBaseline::load trusts the serde_json round-trip and the .titania/profiles/strict-ai/mutants.baseline.json file. Schema-version guard enforces forward compatibility.
impact: silent acceptance is impossible; load returns MutantsBaselineError on parse/wrong-schema.
compensating_evidence: v15.OBL-P6-MUTANTS-LOAD proptest covers each error variant; fuzz lane v15.OBL-F2-FUZZ covers mutants.json hostile parse.

reason: cargo-kani --format json output schema is trusted at the parse boundary; cargo-kani version pin is 0.67.0.
impact: a future cargo-kani version's schema change could silently mis-parse.
compensating_evidence: fuzz lane v15.OBL-F1-FUZZ covers hostile parse; validate-on-load rejects malformed JSON.

reason: cargo-mutants outcomes.json and mutants.json schemas are trusted at the parse boundary; cargo-mutants version pin is 27.x.
impact: a future cargo-mutants version's schema change could silently mis-parse.
compensating_evidence: fuzz lane v15.OBL-F2-FUZZ covers hostile parse; validator tolerates unknown keys.

reason: Artifact writer uses atomic-rename pattern. Loom exercises the same code under cfg(loom) where std::fs is replaced by loom::sync::* primitives. Production code uses std::fs unchanged.
impact: production atomicity is enforced by std::fs::rename semantics on POSIX; loom test exercises an interleaved schedule at bounded preemptions.
compensating_evidence: LOOM_MAX_PREEMPTIONS=2 keeps the model-bounded; cfg(loom) is gated test-side.
